system_prompt:
You are an autonomous Slay the Spire 2 agent playing a complete run. You make every decision — combat plays, map routing, card rewards, shop purchases, rest sites, events — to maximize your chance of defeating the Act 3 boss.

## Core Game Rules
- **Turn structure**: Each turn you draw 5 cards, gain 3 energy, and your Block resets to 0. Play cards (costs energy), use potions (free). At end of turn, all remaining hand cards are discarded.
- **Hand resets every turn**: You get 5 NEW cards each turn from your draw pile. Cards with Retain stay.
- **Block resets every turn**: Block only protects you during the upcoming enemy turn. You cannot stockpile it.
- **Energy resets to 3**: Unspent energy is wasted. Use ALL your energy each turn.
- **HP is a run-wide resource**: Every point of HP lost in non-boss fights is HP you won't have for the boss. Even at high HP, cherish every single point — avoid ANY unnecessary damage. If playing defensively for 1-2 extra rounds lets you take zero damage instead of 10, always choose the slower but lossless line. Only trade HP for speed when enemies scale dangerously (Strength stacking, summons).
- **HP fully restores between Acts**: After beating an Act boss, you heal to full. Boss fights: play to win, not to save HP.
- **Enemy intents are visible**: Attack (damage value shown), Defend, Buff, Debuff, Status (adds junk cards to your deck).
- **Draw pile cycles**: When empty, discard pile shuffles back. Check draw pile to predict next turn's hand.

## Card & Deck Philosophy
- Evaluate cards along 4 dimensions: **Damage** (kill faster), **Defense** (survive), **Draw** (cycle deck faster), **Energy** (play more per turn).
- A strong deck needs all 4 dimensions in balance. Identify which dimension your deck lacks most.

## Strategic Priorities
- **Rest sites**: Upgrade (Smith) by default — an upgrade improves every remaining combat permanently. Only rest when HP is too low to survive the next fight given your deck's defensive capability.
- **Shops**: Choose whatever gives the biggest power spike for remaining fights — cards, relics, removal, or potions.
- **Potions**: Dying with full potions is the worst outcome. Instant potions (Fire, Block) are safe anytime. Sustained-buff potions (Strength, Dexterity, Regen) provide 3-4x more value in long boss/elite fights — check Potion Strategy section. Use assess_potion_value tool when unsure.

## Available Query Tools
You can look up information before making decisions. Use these when you need details:
- **recall_encounter**: Remember past fights against a specific enemy — what worked and what didn't.
- **get_run_progress**: Get a summary of your run so far — HP trend, deck evolution, key decisions.
- **search_strategy**: Find relevant strategy advice (e.g., "how to beat Act 2 boss", "when to take elite fights").
- **read_guide**: Read character-specific or boss-specific strategy guides.
- **assess_potion_value**: Analyze whether to use a sustained-buff potion now or save it for boss/elite.

Enemy info and card details are provided in the game state — don't waste tool calls looking them up. Use recall_encounter for past combat experience, search_strategy or read_guide for boss/strategy advice.

## Decision Tools
After gathering information, use the appropriate decision tool to commit your choice. Exactly one decision tool per turn.

**IMPORTANT**: You have a maximum of 5 query rounds before you MUST call a decision tool. Don't spend all rounds on lookups — 1-2 queries is usually enough. If you have enough context from the game state and your memory, skip queries and decide immediately.

## Strategic Notes — Build Plan
Every non-combat decision tool has a `strategic_note` field. Write a RUNNING BUILD PLAN:
1. **Win condition**: How do you kill bosses? (e.g., "Poison stacking via Noxious Fumes + Catalyst burst")
2. **Key pieces**: Cards that define your build
3. **Gaps**: What's missing? (e.g., "No draw engine yet")
4. **Avoid**: What doesn't fit your win condition
Keep under 50 words. Revise when you take or skip a pivotal card. This note persists across ALL future decisions this run.
Example: "Win: Strength scaling (Demon Form). Key: Demon Form, Pummel. Gap: draw engine, need Offering/Battle Trance. Avoid: expensive non-scaling attacks."

messages.content:
## Combat Start
Enemies: Inklet + Inklet + Inklet
- Inklet [index=0]: HP 12/12, Block 0 | powers: Slippery(1): The next time this creature loses HP, it only loses 1 HP instead.
- Inklet [index=1]: HP 15/15, Block 0 | powers: Slippery(1): The next time this creature loses HP, it only loses 1 HP instead.
- Inklet [index=2]: HP 13/13, Block 0 | powers: Slippery(1): The next time this creature loses HP, it only loses 1 HP instead.

Player HP: 42/70 | Block: 0 | Energy: 3/3

## Current Deck (15 cards)
  [Attack] Strike x4, Backstab(cost=0), Neutralize(cost=0)
  [Skill] Defend x5, Snakebite++ x2, Bubble Bubble(cost=1), Survivor(cost=1)

## Relics (2)
- Ring of the Snake: At the start of each combat, draw 2 additional cards.
- Golden Pearl: Upon pickup, gain 150 Gold.

## Strategic Thread
*Your deck-building decisions so far — fight with this deck's strengths.*

- [rest_site] Win: Poison scaling (Snakebite+) + chip damage. Key: Snakebite, Neutralize, Survivor. Gap: draw engine, more poison sources. Avoid: adding bloat cards. Upgrade Snakebite now for energy relief — critical bottleneck fix.
- [card_select] Win: Poison scaling (Snakebite+10 → Noxious Fumes → Catalyst). Key: Snakebite+, Backstab, Neutralize. Gap: draw engine, removal of 9+ basics, still need Noxious Fumes/Catalyst. Avoid: non-poison bloat.
- [map] Win: Poison/shiv synergy. Key: Snakebite, Neutralize. Gap: Need 2-3 more non-basic damage cards before elites — deck still ~60% basics. Avoid: elites until deck power improves.
- [card_reward] Win: Poison scaling (2x Snakebite + Noxious Fumes → Catalyst burst). Key: Snakebite++, Snakebite, Backstab, Neutralize. Gap: draw engine, removal of 9+ basics. Avoid: non-poison bloat, expensive cards.
- [map] Win: Poison/shiv burst via Snakebite + future poison cards. Key: Snakebite, Neutralize, Survivor. Gap: No scaling engine yet, deck too basic (need Noxious Fumes or shiv generators). Avoid: elites until deck has 3+ non-basic damage sources.
- [rest_site] Win: poison + chip via Snakebite/Neutralize. Key: Bubble Bubble (free block), Snakebite++. Gap: draw engine, energy relief. Priority now: upgrade Bubble Bubble to 0-cost for energy efficiency. Avoid: expensive non-scaling cards.
- [card_select] Win: Poison scaling (Snakebite+/++ → Bubble Bubble → Catalyst burst). Key: Snakebite+ (now 10 poison), Snakebite++, Bubble Bubble, Backstab. Gap: draw engine, card removal urgency HIGH (11 basics). Avoid: non-poison bloat.

## Strategy Skills
## Expert Knowledge (retrieved skills)
*Apply these strategies to the current situation. Deviate only with good reason.*


**Core Combat Principles** (99%)
Every combat turn: (1) Read enemy intents — if ALL enemies have non-attack intents (Buff/Debuff/Sleep), it is a FREE offense window. In multi-enemy fights, some enemies may still attack while others buff — always account for total incoming damage and block accordingly. (2) Dead enemies deal zero damage. If your attacks can kill an enemy this turn, prioritize that. (3) Check your draw pile — knowing what cards remain tells you what you might draw next turn or from draw effects. Plan your plays considering both current hand and future draws. (4) Use ALL energy — unspent energy is permanently wasted. If you have 1 energy left, play a 1-cost card. (5) Multi-enemy: focus fire the most dangerous attacker first. Each kill reduces incoming damage next turn. Use AoE when 3+ enemies alive. (6) Potions don't cost energy — use buff potions BEFORE attacks, damage potions to secure kills, defensive potions vs lethal incoming.
  - Example: Single enemy buffs → play all attacks. Multi-enemy: one enemy buffs but others attack → still block for the attackers

**Energy management - avoid dead turns** (99%)
Before ending your turn, check if you have 0 energy remaining and cards still in hand. If you're about to pass a turn with no plays, this is CRITICAL - you're wasting a turn and taking damage for nothing.

Prevention strategies:
- Always play at least one card per turn if possible
- If hand is expensive (all cards cost 2+), prioritize cheaper cards in deck building
- Consider using potions to generate energy or draw cheaper cards
- If you must pass, ensure you have strong block from previous turn or will survive the hit

Passing turns with no plays is almost always a losing position.

**Multi-enemy combat: offense windows and kill priority** (98%)
In multi-enemy fights, offense is survival. Turtling behind block loses the attrition war because total incoming damage exceeds your block capacity every turn.

**Offense windows -- non-attack intents are FREE turns**:
- Before allocating energy, read ALL enemy intents. Non-attack intents (Buff, Debuff, Stun, Sleep) deal ZERO damage
- If total incoming is <=8: FREE OFFENSE TURN. Play at most 1 block card. Spend remaining energy on attacks targeting one enemy
- If only 1 of 3 enemies is attacking: block for that enemy only, spend remaining energy on offense

**Kill priority -- reduce total DPT permanently**:
- Focus fire the enemy with the best (damage_per_turn / HP) ratio. Usually the lower-HP, higher-damage enemy
- Dead enemies deal zero damage. Each kill reduces incoming by 8-15/turn
- Split damage across enemies is almost always wrong

**Anti-turtling check (after round 2)**:
- If no enemy is below 50% HP after 2 rounds, you are in a DEATH SPIRAL
- Total enemy DPT stays constant while your HP decreases each turn
- Pure blocking only delays death by 1-2 turns. Immediately switch to all-offense targeting the squishiest enemy

**Early game survival priority** (95%)
Floors 1-3: ALWAYS use early_game_survival_gate before finalizing any plan when HP<=30 or incoming>
...(truncated)

## Past Combat Experience
## Enemy Intel
*Adapt these insights to the current situation.*

- [Guide: multi:Inklet+Inklet+Inklet] - Neutralize turn 1 remains optimal — all 12 wins opened with it. Weak on one Inklet immediately cuts chip damage; even small reductions compound over 4-5 rounds against 3 attackers.
- Noxious Fumes is the fastest path to victory when available — the 3 quickest wins (3-4 rounds, minimal HP loss) all used Fumes. AoE poison triples value against 3 targets and frees energy for pure blocking.
- Block-heavy is mandatory — Defend appeared 62 times across wins vs Strike's 25. Spend most energy on defense; only attack to apply poison/Weak or finish low-HP Inklets. The +0 HP win used Cloak and Dagger + Follow Through for massive block turns.
- Survivor (16 plays) smooths consistency — discard dead Strikes late-fight to cycle into Defends, maintaining block density as damage accumulates.
- Without Fumes, burst down one Inklet early with Backstab/Blade Dance to reduce incoming damage from 3 sources to 2. The -7 HP win used this approach successfully.

**Past Encounters:**
- Past: vs Soul Fysh (17 rounds): WON, cards played: 55
- Past: vs multi:Inklet+Inklet+Inklet (5 rounds): WON, cards played: 16


## Potion Strategy
Fight: monster | Boss: 8 floors away
- Potion of Binding [INSTANT]: Apply 1 Weak and 1 Vulnerable to ALL enemies.
- Swift Potion [INSTANT]: Draw 3 cards.

## Combat Rules
- Only play cards marked [PLAYABLE]. Cards marked [UNPLAYABLE] will fail.
- Cards show `cost=N` — you need at least N energy remaining to play them.
- Include potions in your combat plan when useful (they don't cost energy).
- **Discard effects**: If a card requires discarding (e.g. Survivor), specify with the `"discard"` field. Example: `{"type": "card", "card": "Survivor", "target_index": -1, "discard": "Defend"}`
- **Target priority**: Kill enemies that scale (Strength-stacking, buffing). Against non-scaling enemies, block every attack to minimize HP loss.

---

## Computed Insights
- multi_enemy_incoming_damage: total_incoming=8, net_damage=8, hp_after=34, survives=True, recommendation=BLOCK_OPTIMAL, note=8 incoming. Best block: Survivor (8 block) — fully covers. Use remaining energy for offense.
- silent_survival_check: verdict=GO
- buffer_survival_check: Survive: YES, 30hp left
- playable_cards_check: playable_count=7, playable_cards=['Backstab', 'Defend', 'Defend', 'Defend', 'Survivor', 'Defend', 'Strike'], unplayable_cards=[]

## Round 1 State
Energy: 3/3 | HP: 42/70 | Block: 0

## Enemies
- Inklet [index=0]: HP 12/12, Block 0, Intent: Attack(3) | powers: Slippery(1): The next time this creature loses HP, it only loses 1 HP instead.
- Inklet [index=1]: HP 15/15, Block 0, Intent: Attack(2x3=6) | powers: Slippery(1): The next time this creature loses HP, it only loses 1 HP instead.
- Inklet [index=2]: HP 13/13, Block 0, Intent: Attack(3) | powers: Slippery(1): The next time this creature loses HP, it only loses 1 HP instead.

Incoming damage: 12 (after block: 12) | Your HP: 42

## Hand (7 playable / 7 total)
- Backstab (cost=0) [11 dmg] -> targets enemies: Innate. Deal 11 damage. Exhaust.
    vs Inklet[0]: 1 dmg
    vs Inklet[1]: 1 dmg
    vs Inklet[2]: 1 dmg
- Defend (cost=1) [5 block]: Gain 5 Block.
- Defend (cost=1) [5 block]: Gain 5 Block.
- Defend (cost=1) [5 block]: Gain 5 Block.
- Survivor (cost=1) [8 block]: Gain 8 Block. Discard 1 card.
- Defend (cost=1) [5 block]: Gain 5 Block.
- Strike (cost=1) [6 dmg] -> targets enemies: Deal 6 damage.
    vs Inklet[0]: 1 dmg
    vs Inklet[1]: 1 dmg
    vs Inklet[2]: 1 dmg
!! DISCARD: Survivor will require discarding. Fill the "discard" field in your plan.

## Key Effects (active this combat)
- Block: Absorbs damage until your next turn, then resets to 0.
- Exhaust: Removed from combat permanently after use. Thins deck mid-fight.
- Innate: Always in your opening hand.

## Piles
Piles: Draw 5 | Discard 0 | Exhaust 0
Draw (5): Bubble Bubble, Defend, Neutralize, Snakebite+, Strike

## Usable Potions
- [potion_index=0] Potion of Binding [INSTANT]: Apply 1 Weak and 1 Vulnerable to ALL enemies.
- [potion_index=1] Swift Potion [INSTANT]: Draw 3 cards.

Energy budget: 3E available, fixed-cost total: 6E

CRITICAL RULES:
- Energy RESETS to full each turn. Unspent energy is WASTED.
- Hand cards are DISCARDED at end of turn. Unplayed cards are WASTED.
- 0-cost cards (Shivs, etc.) are FREE — ALWAYS play them.

tools.name: recall_encounter
tools.description: Recall past combat experiences against a specific enemy. Returns strategies that worked or failed, damage patterns, and lessons learned from previous runs.

enemy_name.description: Name of the enemy to recall experiences for.

character.description: Optional: filter by character (e.g., 'Ironclad', 'Silent'). Omit to search across all characters.

tools.name: search_strategy
tools.description: Search for relevant strategy advice from the skill library and knowledge base. Use for tactical questions like 'how to handle Act 2 elites' or 'when to take Offering'.

query.description: Natural language strategy question or topic to search for.

category.description: Optional: filter by category. One of: combat, deck_building, map, rest, shop, event, boss, character.

tools.name: read_guide
tools.description: Read a strategy guide. Topics: character names for build strategy, 'combat'/'fight'/'boss' for combat guides, 'deck'/'build'/'card' for deck guides, 'route'/'map' for pathing guides. Examples: 'ironclad', 'silent', 'combat_boss', 'deck_build', 'route_map'.

topic.description: Guide topic. Use character name for character guides (e.g. 'ironclad', 'silent'), include 'combat'/'boss'/'fight' for combat guides, 'deck'/'build'/'card' for deck guides, 'route'/'map' for route guides.

tools.name: assess_potion_value
tools.description: Analyze the opportunity cost of using a potion now vs saving it. Returns value comparison between current fight and upcoming boss/elite. Use when deciding whether to use a sustained-buff potion (Strength, Dexterity, Focus) in a non-boss fight.

potion_name.description: Name of the potion to analyze.

tools.name: combat_plan
tools.description: Plan the full combat turn: cards to play and potions to use, in order.

plan.description: Ordered sequence of actions. Each is either a card play or potion use.

type.description: 'card' to play a hand card, 'potion' to use a potion.

card.description: Card name (for type=card only). Not used for potions.

potion_index.description: Potion slot index (for type=potion only). Not used for cards.

target_index.description: Target enemy index. Use -1 if no target needed.

discard.description: Card name to discard when this card requires discarding (e.g. Survivor). Omit if card has no discard effect.

end_turn.description: Whether to end turn after executing the plan.

note_to_future_self.description: One sentence for future rounds about combat strategy. E.g. 'Poison at 15, survive 2 more rounds.' or 'Save Catalyst for when Vulnerable lands.'

metadata.scenario: F9 Inklet x3 with Slippery(1), The Silent HP 42/70
metadata.expected_correct_plan: Strike (pop Slippery) → Backstab (11 real dmg) → Survivor + Defend (13 block covers 12 incoming)
metadata.actual_wrong_plan: Backstab (pop, 1 dmg, Exhaust wasted) → Survivor + 3x Defend (23 block, 11 excess)
metadata.reasoning_error: Model treats 0-cost Backstab as free for Slippery pop, ignoring Exhaust = permanent 10 dmg loss. Also over-blocks.
